sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The white square at the top center changed to green (N). So clicking on white squares turns them green. This is the interaction mechanic. Now I need to understand the goal. Let me look at the bottom-left panel's purple pattern to understand which squares need to be clicked.
The bottom-left panel has purple dots in a pattern. The bottom-right grid has a 3x3 arrangement of 3x3 blocks. The white squares are at positions:
- Top center (row 49-51, col 29-31) - now turned green
- Middle left (row 54-56, col 24-26)
- Middle right (row 54-56, col 34-36)
- Bottom center (row 59-61, col 29-31)
The purple pattern in the bottom-left panel is:
```
....pp....
....pp....
..........
pp....pp..
pp....pp..
..........
....pp....
....pp....
```
This is an 8x8 pattern. The purple dots are at:
- Rows 0-1, cols 4-5 (top center)
- Rows 3-4, cols 0-1 and cols 6-7 (middle left and right)
- Rows 6-7, cols 4-5 (bottom center)
This matches the white square positions! The pattern is:
- Top center: purple → white square (now clicked, turned green)
- Middle left: purple → white square
- Middle right: purple → white square  
- Bottom center: purple → white square
So the goal is to click all white squares that correspond to purple dots in the pattern. Since I've already clicked the top center, I need to click the other three: middle left, middle right, and bottom center.
Let me click all three remaining white squares.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the remaining 3 white squares: middle left, middle right, bottom center
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25},  # middle left
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35},  # middle right
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30},  # bottom center
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
